name: Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail
name_original: Llanos de las Américas - circular desde la Aliseda
description: This easy 4.2-mile (6.8 km) hike in Despeñaperros Natural Park takes you through mixed forests to the La Campana River.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Elena offers a diverse landscape, characterized by rugged terrain, river valleys, and historical sites. The region features a network of trails that traverse natural parks and provide views of impressive rock formations and dense woodlands. Hikers can explore paths ranging from gentle riverside walks to more challenging ascents through hills. Best hiking trails around Santa Elena The most popular hiking route is Salto del Fraile desde Miranda del Rey - circular by Despeñaperros natural park, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views within the natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail, an easy 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This trail follows the river, providing a gentle walk through a picturesque gorge. Local hikers also love the Castro Ferral Castle Loop, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Santa Elena is defined by river valleys, natural park landscapes, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding hikes. The routes in Santa Elena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 390 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Elena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Elena?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Santa Elena, offering a variety of experiences. These trails traverse natural parks, river valleys, and historical sites, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes in Santa Elena?
A: Hiking around Santa Elena features diverse terrain, from rugged landscapes and river valleys to dense woodlands. You'll find paths ranging from gentle riverside walks to more challenging ascents through hills, often within natural park settings.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, Santa Elena offers several easy routes. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987608047/los-estrechos-de-miranda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.2-mile (6.8 km) path that follows the river through a picturesque gorge, perfect for a gentle walk. Overall, there are 7 easy routes available.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more demanding experience, Santa Elena has challenging routes. One notable difficult hike is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prehistoric Rock Paintings – Collado de la Aviación Shelter loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10 miles (16.2 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the trails?
A: The trails around Santa Elena offer numerous points of interest. You can explore viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or discover natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Batán&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, many routes in Santa Elena are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987627466/salto-del-fraile-desde-miranda-del-rey-circular-by-despenaperros-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile desde Miranda del Rey - circular by Despeñaperros natural park&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.1-mile (8.3 km) trail offering scenic views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa Elena?
A: The hiking routes in Santa Elena are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to explore natural parks and historical sites.

Q: How long do hikes in Santa Elena typically take?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987608047/los-estrechos-de-miranda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour 50 minutes, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983421627/castro-ferral-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Ferral Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 38 minutes.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to castles or historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in historical points of interest. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983421627/castro-ferral-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Ferral Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied terrain to historical sites, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or gorges?
A: Yes, Santa Elena features several trails that follow rivers and pass through picturesque gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987608047/los-estrechos-de-miranda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a gentle walk alongside the river through a beautiful gorge.
tips: The Llano de las Américas Visitors Center is an innovative facility designed to receive and guide visitors who annually decide to discover the Despeñaperros Natural Park and the Cascada de la Cimbarra Natural Area.
It is an old brick oven that is still standing next to the farmhouse wall, and was mainly used to bake bread.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a gentle descent along a well-defined path, leading towards the La Campana River. You'll walk through a mixed forest, characterized by cork oaks, holm oaks, and areas of pine repopulation. The path is generally easy to navigate.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a journey through diverse forest landscapes. Key points of interest include the Llano de las Américas, a beautiful forest area, and the Las Américas Farmhouse and Moorish Oven, a historical site showcasing past rural life. You'll also encounter the lush riverine vegetation along the La Campana River.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail connects from the Llano de las Américas visitor center. While specific parking details aren't provided, visitor centers in natural parks typically offer parking facilities. It's advisable to check with Despeñaperros Natural Park for the most current information on trailhead access and parking.

faqs.question: Is the Los Estrechos de Miranda Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within Despeñaperros Natural Park, dogs are generally permitted but typically must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check the park's official regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: Given its location in a natural park and the presence of mixed forests, spring and autumn are generally ideal. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and potentially rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Despeñaperros Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Despeñaperros Natural Park typically does not require an entrance fee for general access to its trails. However, specific activities or guided tours might have associated costs. Permits are generally not needed for hiking this public use trail, but it's always good practice to confirm with the park's official visitor center for any updated regulations.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Los Estrechos de Miranda, Transandalus, Río de la Campana, Sendero de Sierra Morena, Camino del Barranco del Lobo, Estrechos, Camino Cortijo de Las Américas, Sendero de Juano, La Salguerita, Estrechos de Miranda, El Zorro, GR48-Sierra Morena, La Pedrera, Arroyo de los Castaños, Las Piedras, Celedonio, and Sendero del Balneario trails, offering opportunities for extended exploration within the park.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: With its 'easy' difficulty rating and gentle descent, this trail is generally suitable for families. The mixed forest environment and historical features like the Moorish oven can make it an engaging experience for children. Always consider your children's fitness levels and the weather conditions.

faqs.question: What is the significance of the Moorish oven along the trail?
faqs.answer: The well-preserved Moorish oven at the Las Américas Farmhouse and Moorish Oven is an important ethnographic element. It's a vestige of past rural life, highlighting how communities in the area once relied on self-sufficiency for food, offering a glimpse into the region's history and traditional practices.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: As a natural park trail, dedicated restrooms or water refill stations directly on the route are unlikely. It's best to use facilities at the Llano de las Américas visitor center before starting and carry sufficient water for your hike. Always pack out what you pack in.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Even for an easy trail, it's wise to bring sturdy walking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a small first-aid kit. Depending on the season, a light jacket or rain gear might also be useful. Don't forget your camera to capture the natural beauty!

waypoints.name: Las Américas Farmhouse and Moorish Oven
